_comments.purpose: Source-of-truth spec for the app's motion system. Read at runtime by /motion-docs and translatable to Lottie .json files via a future export script.
_comments.lottie_translation: Each demo's keyframes correspond to a single layer's transform animation at 60fps over duration_ms. Frame number = duration_ms * fps / 1000 * t. Easing names map to standard Lottie bezier control points (in/out arrays).
_comments.composition_note: All demos render in a 1080x1080 @ 60fps composition with the referenceShape centered. translateX/translateY values in keyframes are in composition pixels — scale them down proportionally when previewing at smaller sizes (player divides by composition.width / preview.width).
_comments.scale_opacity_note: scale and opacity are scale-invariant — they apply identically at any preview size.

demos.title: Tap feedback — button
demos.subtitle: 150ms · ease-out · scale 0.96
demos.description: Buttons and icon buttons scale to 0.96 on press. Fast, responsive, immediate. Used for any primary or secondary CTA, icon controls, FAB.

demos.title: Press feedback — standard card
demos.subtitle: 200ms · ease-out · scale 0.985
demos.description: Standard cards (event cards, list items) get a much subtler press scale than buttons. Used on Perfil and Explorar event cards, unselected radio cards.

demos.title: Press feedback — selected card
demos.subtitle: 200ms · ease-out · scale 0.99
demos.description: Already-lifted cards (selected state) press even less — they're already raised, so the affordance is reduced. Used for selected radio cards.

demos.title: Reveal — popup/emphasis
demos.subtitle: 200ms · ease-out · y:32→0, scale 0.98→1, opacity 0→1
demos.description: Used for FansPill entry, selected card emphasis, popup/sheet content. Soft entry with slight upward translation.

demos.title: Container transition — page/modal
demos.subtitle: 300ms · spring curve · x:48→0, opacity 0→1
demos.description: Page transitions and modal enter/exit. The spring curve (0.22, 1, 0.36, 1) is the ONLY easing that uses a slight overshoot. Containers only — never on interactive controls.

demos.title: Hero moment — illustration entry
demos.subtitle: 800ms · ease-out · scale 0.7→1.05→1, opacity 0→1
demos.description: Reserved for Lottie illustrations on Ramificação and Conclusão — the longer duration carries emotional weight, the kind a map flyTo would carry in a map-based app.

demos.title: ❌ ease-in-out on interactive
demos.subtitle: Feels sluggish at start, drags at end
demos.description: Symmetric ramp makes taps feel mushy. Always ease-out for visible UI.

demos.title: ❌ scale 0.94 on cards
demos.subtitle: Overly aggressive — feels cheap, not premium
demos.description: Anything below 0.96 on buttons or 0.97 on cards reads as cheap. Premium = restraint.

demos.title: ❌ bare transition-colors (no duration)
demos.subtitle: Defaults to 150ms ease-in-out — wrong on both axes
demos.description: Tailwind transition-colors without duration uses default ease-in-out 150ms. Always specify both.

demos.title: ❌ spring curve on interactive control
demos.subtitle: Overshoot on a tap reads as wobbly, not responsive
demos.description: The spring curve is for containers only. On buttons it makes the press feel bouncy and unstable.

demos.title: List stagger — card entrance
demos.subtitle: 5 cards · 50ms stagger · 100ms delay · each 200ms ease-out
demos.description: Event card lists on Perfil and Explorar wave in on mount. Wave, not burst — the stagger creates a deliberate cascade.

demos.title: Radio selection — state change
demos.subtitle: Background morph 200ms + dot fill 200ms — synchronous
demos.description: Selection state on Ramificação. Two things change at once: background color AND inner dot fill. Both 200ms ease-out, both fire synchronously in the handler.

demos.title: Hero conclusion — choreographed
demos.subtitle: Illustration 800ms hero + title reveal 200ms (staggered by 400ms)
demos.description: Conclusão screen: the Lottie hero plays 800ms; the title text reveals 400ms into the hero, not after. One dominant motion with a supporting layer, not sequential.
